evidence: The two files only contain package-local contract metadata and catalog-builder helpers with no imports and no calls into external packages.
All contract identifiers are declared centrally in `protocol_contracts.go` and `startram_contracts.go`, and catalog entries are constructed directly from package-local constants/types.
There is no visible import-time registration, mutable global state, or initialization side-effect in either file.
cross_module_architecture.issues_preventing_higher_score: No material boundary regression is present, but adding a compile-time/registry-level test to enforce completeness of contract-ID usage could increase assurance without changing architecture.

evidence: `runServer` in `main.go` wires the websocket listener address as a hardcoded `":3000"` instead of deriving it from startup/runtime input (`appStartupOptions.httpPort`, `StartupOptions`, or a runtime seam), so caller code cannot fully control or swap server transport endpoints.
`bootstrapRuntime` in `main.go` already treats HTTP listen as configurable through the startup options, but the websocket side channel is not modeled as part of the same abstraction boundary.
abstraction_fitness.issues_preventing_higher_score: Runtime seam exposes HTTP port configurability but not websocket bind contract, creating an inconsistent and partially configurable server abstraction that makes deployment and orchestration harder to adapt.

evidence: `README.md` states contract surfaces should have paired tests and path-aware contract gate coverage for changed catalog entries and lifecycle assertions.

The reviewed contract files are definition-only (`protocol_contracts.go`, `startram_contracts.go`) with no direct deterministic contract assertions in this scope to validate descriptor/message/version behavior on each change.

findings.summary: Server abstraction is partially leaky: websocket bind address is hardcoded to `:3000`, preventing callers from controlling both listeners through the startup/runtime seam.
evidence: In `main.go`, `runServer` creates HTTP server with runtime-configured `httpPort` but creates websocket server with `Addr: ":3000"`.
`main.go` bootstrap path already passes startup configuration into `runBootstrapSubsystems`, yet websocket binding is not represented in those options.
findings.suggestion: Add websocket listener bind address/port to startup options and/or the server runtime contract, thread it through bootstrap startup options, and use that value in `runServer` (with default `":3000"` for backward compatibility). Consider validating both endpoints and supporting a single configurable listener for environments that require unified port bindings.

findings.summary: Protocol and StarTram contract definitions can change without adjacent deterministic contract assertions in this package.
evidence: `README.md` explicitly requires runtime contract gate coverage and deterministic per-catalog assertions for changed contract entries.

`goseg/protocol/contracts/protocol_contracts.go` and `goseg/protocol/contracts/startram_contracts.go` currently only define constants/metadata entries and do not include in-package tests that prove compatibility policy, namespace binding, and message semantics for these descriptors.
findings.suggestion: Add/extend `goseg/protocol/contracts/contracts_test.go` with table-driven tests covering each contract in both files: ID format, namespace/action alignment, descriptor metadata presence, compatibility policy (`contractMetadataFor` output), and masked-error message stability for StarTram. Add targeted tests in CI path checks if behavior changes should fail fast.
